mutations.description: Proceed with initialization when the caller or timer pointer is null.
mutations.find: if (driver == NULL || timer == NULL) return false;
mutations.replace: if (driver == NULL || timer == NULL) return true;

mutations.description: Fail to clear latched capture, overflow, and compare status before enabling TIMER1.
Seed the compare register with an unexpected nonzero value.
Enable TIMER1 before clearing status and programming the initial compare value.
Allow zero and ambiguous half-range compare delays.
Arm a compare without preserving the foreground interrupt boundary.
Program a future compare while an unacknowledged timer event can invalidate the high word.
Use the delay as a raw compare register value instead of adding it to the current counter.
Write a compare target without enabling the compare interrupt source.
Lose the current low-word count while constructing the absolute compare deadline.
Assign simultaneous capture/overflow events to the wrong high-word epoch.
Acknowledge overflow without advancing the software timestamp epoch.
Publish a capture timestamp using zero rather than the hardware-latched count.
Replace a pending capture instead of retaining it and accounting for overrun.
Turn a stale compare status into a terminal compare result without an armed deadline.
Publish a compare result while leaving the repeating hardware source armed.
Leave the status snapshot latched after ISR handling.
Return a capture timestamp without clearing its one-sample latch.
Restore compare result consumption with interrupts enabled instead of the opaque saved state.
Expose the same capture-overrun count repeatedly instead of atomically consuming it.
